user_message: Pulling in context from Humongous Insurance's email thread -- need to identify the blockers for the deployment blockers.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-28 09:38 EST
Subject: URGENT: deployment blockers Issue at Humongous Insurance

There's been a change in our timeline for deployment blockers. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-23 11:20 EST
Subject: Re: deployment blockers Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about deployment blockers. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-08 13:35 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Humongous Insurance deployment blockers

Our team encountered some challenges with deployment blockers. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Humongous Insurance: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-H trap (Over-summarizes -- loses thread-specific nuance); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-H failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-H failure mode (Over-summarizes -- loses thread-specific nuance)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-H -- Over-summarizes -- loses thread-specific nuance
